questions.stem: A company on the Starter plan has 3 projects and 5 team members. They want to add connectors for SharePoint and advanced analytics dashboards. What must they do?

options.text: Enable connectors in workspace settings -- they are available on all plans
Upgrade to at least the Professional plan, which includes connectors, advanced analytics, and supports up to 25 team members
Purchase the connectors add-on separately without changing their plan

questions.explanation: Connectors, advanced analytics, and guardrails are only available on Professional and Enterprise plans. The Starter plan is limited to 2 LLM providers, 3 projects, 5 team members, and lacks these features. Professional supports up to 25 members, 20 projects, all LLM providers, plus connectors and analytics.

questions.stem: A fintech startup notices that one of their agents occasionally consumes their entire monthly token budget in a single day due to long conversation loops. Which combination of Agent Platform features best prevents this?

options.text: Set only a monthly token budget and review usage at month-end
Set a daily token budget to cap daily consumption, enable conversation sliding windows to limit history sent to the LLM, and configure usage threshold alerts at 80%
Switch all agents to the cheapest model available

questions.explanation: The platform recommends multiple cost controls: daily token budgets prevent a single runaway day from consuming the monthly budget; conversation sliding windows limit the conversation history sent to the LLM (reducing input tokens for long sessions); and usage threshold alerts notify you before hitting limits. Simply using the cheapest model may degrade quality without addressing the root cause.

questions.stem: When deploying the Agent Platform with multiple Runtime pods behind a load balancer, session state must be stored in ________ (not in pod-local memory) to ensure any pod can handle any request.
questions.explanation: The Agent Platform follows a stateless distributed architecture. Session state, conversation history, and temporary execution data are stored in Redis (for fast access) and MongoDB (for persistence). This ensures that when a load balancer routes a request to any Runtime pod, that pod has full access to the session context without relying on pod-local memory.

questions.stem: A retail company wants their AI agent available on their website, WhatsApp, and Slack simultaneously. Which approach does the Agent Platform use to support this?

options.text: Build a separate agent for each channel, each with channel-specific logic
Deploy one agent definition and connect it to multiple channel adapters -- the platform handles protocol-specific details so the agent works across all channels without modification
Deploy the agent on the website only and use third-party middleware to bridge to WhatsApp and Slack

questions.explanation: Agent Platform provides channel adapters (Web SDK, Slack, WhatsApp, voice, Teams, Telegram, email) that handle protocol-specific details. Your agent definition works across all channels without modification. Rich content blocks (RICH_CONTENT) let you provide channel-specific formatting while maintaining a single agent codebase.

questions.stem: Which channel would you recommend for a B2B enterprise that wants their customers to interact with an AI agent within Microsoft Teams, using rich interactive elements like booking confirmations with structured data?

options.text: WhatsApp interactive messages with button lists
Microsoft Teams with Adaptive Cards that display structured data like booking details in a formatted card layout
A web SDK widget embedded in the company portal

questions.explanation: For B2B enterprises already using Microsoft Teams, the Teams channel with Adaptive Cards is the natural fit. Adaptive Cards support rich structured layouts (FactSets for booking details, TextBlocks for headers) directly within the Teams interface. While a web widget would work, it adds friction by requiring users to leave Teams.
